af_score_components.notes: VectorCode code repository indexer with MCP. Has MCP quality 70. No auth (local Chroma DB). Tree-sitter semantic chunking + Chroma vector embeddings. Primary integration is Neovim; MCP is secondary. Local Chroma DB must be running. Minor version bumps may indicate breaking changes. auth_complexity=100 for no auth (local only).

security_score_components.security_notes: No authentication — local tool accessing local Chroma DB. All data stays local (good for private codebases). No compliance certifications. Open-source Python codebase. Security depends on host machine security. Chroma DB is local so no network exposure by default. tls_enforcement=60 as external embedding API calls use HTTPS but local components are unencrypted.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local tool — reliability depends on local environment. Chroma DB must be running separately. Initial indexing of large repos time-consuming. Pin versions due to potential breaking changes in minor bumps. No SLA. Active development at v0.7.20. Neovim-first design means MCP interface may lag behind Lua API features.

af_score_components.notes: Vercel deployment management API. No MCP server. Personal access tokens (simple) or OAuth2 for integrations. OpenAPI spec available. Good documentation. Environment variables encrypted at rest and cannot be read back after setting. Each deploy gets unique URL (immutable). auth_complexity=85 for simple PAT with good scoping.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. PAT and OAuth2 options with team-scoped tokens. Environment variables encrypted at rest. SOC2/GDPR compliant. Webhook payload verification available. Deployment URLs are immutable (security benefit). Hobby plan restrictions for commercial use. Strong overall security for a deployment platform.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Vercel has public status page and enterprise SLA. API versioned and stable. Build logs require polling (no push notification). Cursor pagination. Retry guidance documented. Deployment process is idempotent (each deploy gets unique URL). Strong reliability record as a major hosting platform.

af_score_components.notes: Vestige neuroscience-inspired AI agent memory system. Has MCP quality 85 (21 MCP tools). No auth (fully local). FSRS-6 spaced repetition, spreading activation, memory consolidation. SQLCipher optional encryption. First run downloads ~130MB embedding model. AGPL-3.0 license may affect commercial use. auth_complexity=100 for no auth (local only).

security_score_components.security_notes: No authentication — fully local. Optional SQLCipher database encryption is a good security feature. All data stays on device (strong privacy). AGPL-3.0 requires open-sourcing commercial integrations. First-run model download requires internet. Rust implementation provides memory safety. No compliance certifications as personal project.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local tool — reliability is user-managed. Cursor pagination for memory retrieval. Dream consolidation runs during idle periods (variable latency). 21 MCP tools is large surface area. WebSocket dashboard adds separate process management. Active development. No SLA. Suitable for personal AI assistant use; not for production multi-user systems.

af_score_components.notes: Vibe is monday.com's React UI component library with MCP server for AI-assisted development. Has MCP quality 72. No auth required. MCP helps agents accurately use Vibe components without hallucination. Version 3 has breaking changes from v2. Monorepo structure (separate @vibe/core, @vibe/icons packages). auth_complexity=100 for no auth.

security_score_components.security_notes: No authentication — local development tool. Component library itself has no security implications. MCP server provides read-only component documentation access. monday.com maintains the library with regular security updates. No compliance requirements for a UI component library. Open-source codebase auditable.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: monday.com-maintained library with enterprise backing. Regular versioned releases. v3 breaking changes from v2 require migration. MCP server provides idempotent component lookups. No SLA for open-source component library. Active development and maintenance. Storybook documentation provides good component reference.

af_score_components.notes: Vibe Check meta-mentor MCP for AI agents using Chain-Pattern Interrupts. Has MCP quality 82. Requires at least one LLM API key (Gemini recommended). Each vibe_check call incurs LLM API cost. sessionId must be managed by calling agent. Optimal CPI dosage 10-20% of steps. Research claims need independent validation. auth_complexity=85 for env-var LLM API key.

security_score_components.security_notes: API keys via environment variables. Supports multiple LLM providers (more keys = larger secret surface). HTTPS for all LLM API calls. Local MCP server — no data stored externally. Session history stored locally. No compliance certifications. Community project with limited security review. Multiple provider API keys increase management complexity.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Reliability depends on chosen LLM provider availability. Gemini recommended as default due to cost efficiency. Each oversight call adds latency. sessionId management required for history continuity. Local MCP server reliability. No SLA. Active development with research-backed design. Fallback provider support helps reliability.

af_score_components.notes: Vibetest MCP launches concurrent Browser-Use agents for website QA testing. Has MCP quality 65. Requires Google API key (GOOGLE_API_KEY) for gemini-2.0-flash. Python 3.11+ and Playwright Chromium required. Not for structured test suites with assertions. Headless mode behavior varies by environment. auth_complexity=85 for env-var Google API key.

security_score_components.security_notes: Google API key via environment variable. HTTPS for Gemini API calls. Browser automation has inherent security considerations (can interact with any URL). No compliance certifications. Community project from browser-use org. Testing localhost environments requires careful network permissions. No persistent data storage.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Reliability depends on Google Gemini API and Playwright. Browser automation is inherently non-deterministic. Headless mode differences across environments. No SLA. Early-stage tool — not suitable for CI/CD deterministic testing. Good for exploratory QA. Python 3.11+ and Playwright Chromium requirements add setup friction.

af_score_components.notes: Vimeo video hosting API. No MCP server. OAuth2 for user-auth, personal access tokens for server-side. Video uploads use tus-based resumable protocol (not simple POST — requires specialized logic). Transcoding is async (agents must poll or use webhooks). Some endpoints require specific paid plans. auth_complexity=70 for OAuth2 with scopes.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 with granular scopes (video.upload, video.read, video.write, video.delete, etc.). Personal access tokens via Authorization header. GDPR compliant. Privacy controls (password-protected, domain-restricted videos) are a security feature. Webhook delivery for async events. Good overall security for video platform.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Vimeo has status page. Enterprise SLA on business plans. tus resumable uploads handle network interruptions gracefully. Transcoding async with polling or webhooks. Offset pagination. Rate limits per access token. Video processing time varies with length/quality. Reliable for professional video hosting use cases.

af_score_components.notes: IMPORTANT: Viper is a red team/adversary simulation C2 platform intended ONLY for authorized penetration testing. It is NOT an MCP server in the traditional sense — it does not expose MCP tools for external AI agents. Has MCP server quality 20 (minimal/internal). Username/password auth for web dashboard. Illegal use is a serious risk. auth_complexity=70 for username/password web auth.

security_score_components.security_notes: Web dashboard with username/password auth. Self-hosted deployment — security is entirely operator-managed. Docker deployment required. LLM API key configuration needed for AI features. No compliance certifications. This is an offensive security tool — security of the platform itself is secondary to its offensive capabilities. Operator OPSEC is critical.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted — reliability entirely operator-managed. Docker deployment provides some consistency. Active development (v3.1.10). Free alternative to Cobalt Strike but without commercial support. Reliability for red team operations depends on operational environment. No SLA. Community-maintained.

af_score_components.notes: VirusTotal threat intelligence API with MCP server. Has MCP quality 72 (official MCP server). API key via x-apikey header. Free tier 4 req/min limit makes agents extremely slow — throttling mandatory. File submissions are PUBLIC (never submit confidential files). 404 for file hash doesn't mean clean. URL path parameters require base64url encoding. auth_complexity=100 for simple API key.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. API key via dedicated x-apikey header (not Authorization). GDPR compliant. Premium keys unlock additional endpoints. File submissions become publicly visible — critical security consideration for proprietary data. Google-owned (Alphabet) infrastructure ensures enterprise security. No credential exposure in URLs.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Google-owned platform with enterprise reliability. Status page available. Cursor pagination. Retry guidance documented. File analysis is async — polling required for results. Free tier rate limits (4/min) significantly impact agent workflow design. Premium tiers offer better rate limits. OpenAPI reference documentation comprehensive.

af_score_components.notes: VoiceMode MCP server for voice conversations with Claude Code. Has MCP quality 72. Optional OpenAI API key for cloud STT/TTS; fully local option with Whisper + Kokoro (no key needed). Requires FFmpeg, portaudio, and platform-specific audio libraries. Local model download needed (~hundreds of MB first run). WSL on Windows needs audio routing config. auth_complexity=95 for optional API key (can run fully without).

security_score_components.security_notes: Optional OpenAI API key via env var. Fully local mode (Whisper + Kokoro) processes no data externally — strong privacy option. HTTPS for OpenAI API calls when used. Local audio processing on device. No compliance certifications. Community project with limited security review. Privacy-first design with local model option.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local tool — reliability depends on system audio setup. Silence detection may cut off speech in noisy environments. Local model download required first run. Platform-specific audio library dependencies add fragility. WSL requires extra configuration. No SLA. Active development with readthedocs documentation. Not suitable for production voice applications.
